repo.description: Vim-fork focused on extensibility and usability
repo.name: neovim

body:
Allows users to clear all images with a single function call. Uses kitty's d=A command to clear all placements in a single escape sequence rather than N individual deletes, also freeing stored image data not referenced by the scrollback buffer.

Closes #39439 



https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/6528fff9-f19c-4526-9f3e-73cde887b28b

comment_data.body: to avoid accidents we could use `id=-1` to mean "all"? or something like that?
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -94,12 +97,27 @@ function M.get(id)
   return vim.deepcopy(entry.opts)
 end
 
----Delete an image, removing it from display.
+---Delete an image, or all images if no id is given.
 ---
----@param id integer
----@return boolean found true if the image existed
+---@param id? integer image id, or nil to delete all images

comment_data.body:
@justinmk are we expecting IDs to be positive integers? With the integer type, it can be anything. so if a backend like kitty or any future backend gave you an ID of -1, that would break this.

We could do something like `true` instead or pass an opts table like `vim.ui.img.del({ all = true })` to make it very clear. If taking `true`, we'd do something like:

```lua
if type(id) == 'boolean' and id == true then
    -- Do clear all images
end
```
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -94,12 +97,27 @@ function M.get(id)
   return vim.deepcopy(entry.opts)
 end
 
----Delete an image, removing it from display.
+---Delete an image, or all images if no id is given.
 ---
----@param id integer
----@return boolean found true if the image existed
+---@param id? integer image id, or nil to delete all images

comment_data.body: how about `math.huge` ? polymorphic types are a last resort
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -94,12 +97,27 @@ function M.get(id)
   return vim.deepcopy(entry.opts)
 end
 
----Delete an image, removing it from display.
+---Delete an image, or all images if no id is given.
 ---
----@param id integer
----@return boolean found true if the image existed
+---@param id? integer image id, or nil to delete all images

comment_data.body:
It's a better type check since it resolves to `inf`. Feels a little weird to me to do `vim.ui.img.del(math.huge)`, but I'll leave it up to your discretion.

It's not like we'd be able to support that many images generated using only positive integer values for the id, so if you wanted to - since we control the id generation - we could just document that ids will always be non-zero, positive integer values and use the `-1` that you recommended.
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -94,12 +97,27 @@ function M.get(id)
   return vim.deepcopy(entry.opts)
 end
 
----Delete an image, removing it from display.
+---Delete an image, or all images if no id is given.
 ---
----@param id integer
----@return boolean found true if the image existed
+---@param id? integer image id, or nil to delete all images

comment_data.body: `del(math.huge)` seems pretty intuitive, since the intent is to delete everything.
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -94,12 +97,27 @@ function M.get(id)
   return vim.deepcopy(entry.opts)
 end
 
----Delete an image, removing it from display.
+---Delete an image, or all images if no id is given.
 ---
----@param id integer
----@return boolean found true if the image existed
+---@param id? integer image id, or nil to delete all images

comment_data.body:
it's not intuitive at all
It makes much more sense to either just introduce a flag or a separate function `clear`
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -94,12 +97,27 @@ function M.get(id)
   return vim.deepcopy(entry.opts)
 end
 
----Delete an image, removing it from display.
+---Delete an image, or all images if no id is given.
 ---

----@return boolean found true if the image existed
+---@param id? integer image id, or nil to delete all images

comment_data.body:
if it becomes a real problem we can revisit later. not going to debate it now.

> or a separate function `clear`


that function exists, it's called `del`

@@ -94,12 +97,27 @@ function M.get(id)
   return vim.deepcopy(entry.opts)
 end
 
----Delete an image, removing it from display.
+---Delete an image, or all images if no id is given.
 ---

----@return boolean found true if the image existed
+---@param id? integer image id, or nil to delete all images

comment_data.body:
you started the whole debate about using a different way to do this than an intuitive one
and since for some reason you don't like that, I suggested using another function for it with a name clearly describing what it would do
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -94,12 +97,27 @@ function M.get(id)
   return vim.deepcopy(entry.opts)
 end

comment_regular_data.body: @justinmk updated to `math.huge`. Let me know what you think.
